scaled_oracle_cases.description: Main scaled case using seed data, testing bar function directly for stronger verification.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must produce correct free energy and uncertainty for typical normally distributed work values.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The output must match expected numerical values from the reference implementation.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
results = bar(w_F, w_R)
print(f"Free energy difference ΔF = {results['Delta_f']:.3f} ± {results['dDelta_f']:.3f} kT")
result = results
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must provide stronger executable verification coverage for this operator.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Edge case with zero work values to test robustness of the BAR implementation.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must handle the boundary case where all forward and reverse work values are zero, returning zero free energy difference and zero uncertainty.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Input arrays of all zeros should produce Delta_f = 0.0 and dDelta_f = 0.0.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
results = bar(w_F, w_R)
print(f"Free energy difference ΔF = {results['Delta_f']:.3f} ± {results['dDelta_f']:.3f} kT")
result = results
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must provide stronger executable verification coverage for this operator.
